title: triticum aestivum var. albidum
intro: Triticum aestivum var. albidum, also known as white wheat, is a variety of the common wheat plant (Triticum aestivum) characterized by its pale or white-colored kernels. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of white wheat include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and aid in digestion, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. White wheat can be prepared in various forms, including tea, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote overall health and well-being.
